text:
This paper presents the ECCO v4 non-linear inverse modeling framework and its initial assessment of the ECCO v4 baseline solution of the evolving global ocean state from 1992 to 2011. The ECCO v4 framework and its associated baseline solution are fully available to the public and are being continuously tested and validated using an automated regression testing scheme. The ECCO v4 modeling framework uses global conformal grids, multiple data constraints, a variety of control parameters, algorithmic differentiation, and a robust, grid-independent Matlab toolbox (FSW) for solving the optimization problem. The ECCO v4 constraint-based modeling framework has enabled us to compute a solution of the global ocean state estimate that differs from standard data assimilation products in two key ways: (i) the ECCO v4 solution is a dynamically consistent ocean state estimate without any excess heat or buoyancy being added to the ocean. (ii) The reproducibility of the solution by other researchers is fully guaranteed.

The model solution is shown to provide an acceptable fit to most of the observed data within the constraints of physical realism. The modeling system and the state estimate are shown to be intimately linked in the modeling process. The inverse estimation of parameterized physics terms was critical in achieving the better fit to the hydrography, and the optimized parameter values have been incorporated into the standard model configuration. Initial assessment of the structure of model error indicates that parametric and external errors are of similar magnitude and both greatly exceed structural error. A major conclusion of this study is that the turbulent transport parameters should be considered as formal control parameters in the inverse problem.
